rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET BOYD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although Schedule F attached to the amended petition (Securities at Appraised Value) did not include the 135 shares of Still Water Mining appraised at $787.57 so that the total of the Merrill Lynch account did not correctly reflect the I&A values, and the net cash set forth in the supplement to the amended petition does not appear to be correct, if the bank balance was $83,632.48, but reduced by the as yet outstanding creditor's claim of $830.42 and reduced further by the $20,000 "reserve", the $6,975.60 partial attorney statutory fee, and $2,727.00 reimbursement of costs to the attorney, which should result in net cash of $53,099.46, not $52,665.95. The submitted proposed order showed the assets (securities) as of the date of death. Any future notice must be given to the creditor, SNP Pharmacy, until such allowed claim is paid. Status of closing the estate is set for 1/26/2022. A petition for final distribution or for additional time for estate administration must be filed by no later than 1/12/2022. Appearance required on 1/26/2022 if not filed. (There is no need to give notice of a supplement. [Probate Code Sect. 1205 and CRC 7.53(b).] Furthermore, a Civil version of a proof of service attached to a pleading in Probate is inadequate if notice were required for that pleading. Only the mandatory Judicial Council forms of notice may be used in Probate proceedings.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MARGARET E. LOPRESTI 1998 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/1/21 for CIU report to be done (earlier than scheduled in order to synchronize trust account with investigation of conservatorship of beneficiary). The related case in the petition caption is the incorrect case number. The petition includes fees and costs also in connection with the conservatorship case, which should have been requested in that case even if they are payable by the trustee. Household goods were sold on 11/12/20, but none was inventoried. Why were there "Conserv. Fees Owed to Trust" reflected on Schedule A on 3/19/19? Who was consulted re reverse mortgage on 2/13/20 for $150 paid to the trustee? The cash on hand far exceeds the FDIC insured amount in one bank account. It appears that a liabilities schedule should have been included to reflect fees ordered on 12/18/19 and apparently not yet paid..? When were the $1,019.70 trustee fees for "April" paid on 4/30/20 ordered? Attorney time entries mention a "5/28 Court order" on 6/12/20, but there does not appear to be an order dated 5/28/20, and it does not appear that an ex parte petition "re acceptance of language of the order" was filed around July 2020. It is unclear why the estate should bear the cost of two attorneys appearing by CourtCall -- or anyone appearing by CourtCall when Zoom was available for no charge beginning mid-2020. (The fiduciary's time sheets have not been reviewed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/23/21. (There is no need to repeat attorney time entries in a fee declaration when time sheets were submitted as part of the accounting petition. More than $2,600 was billed in connection preparing and filing fee request pleadings.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Prince M. Edmonds, Jr.S Petition For: (1) An Order Against Latonya Evette Carpenter Requiring Return Of Personal Property To Trust, And For Damages And Other Relief Based Upon Financial Elder Abuse And Other Wrongful Conduct; (2) An Order That Terry Thompson Be Suspended And Removed As Temporary Trustee; And (3) An Order That Terry Thompson And Or Jonathan Madison Return Funds Taken From The Trust And Reimburse Petitioner?S Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021 court calendar full. Issues: 1) Examiner received an unfiled courtesy copy of Temporary Successor Trustee's Reply which has been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Examiner sees proposed orders in the filing queue. Attorney Jonathan Madison must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA QUIGLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No courtesy copy of the petition and no proposed order have been provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., 14.7 B. and C., and 14.60 E. and as posted in prior tentative rulings. No proof of service of notice to the sole heir-at-law, Thomas Quigley, has been filed. Despite stating in various papers at the outset of these proceedings that the sole heir-at-law is the decedent's brother, Thomas Quigley, the administrator now claims she is the only "beneficiary" by "the terms of the decedent's will." (There was no will.) The administrator is NOT the party entitled to distribution and there was NO "judgment" or order for any distribution and there was NO waiver of accounting by Thomas Quigley. Thomas Quigley is the only party who may waive an accounting. An accounting for a decedent's estate must begin with the date of death. [LRSF 14.35.] There is no actual accounting attached to the petition, just a "Summary Accounting" that lists incompletely described receipts and expenses and does not meet statutory requirements. [Prob. C. Sect. 1060 et seq.] The real property was appraised at $950,000. It is unclear why the figure $765,000 is used instead. No bank statement has been filed to verify the purported property on hand of $660,803.23. [LRSF 14.35 D.] (And there is no Schedule A or Schedule G, as alleged in the Summary of Account on page 4.) There are multiple other issues besides these (e.g., an odd calculation of the statutory fee and missing allegations required by Prob. C. Sections 1064 and 9202 and LRSF 14.35.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALENTINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Petition To Waive Bond
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) An additional partial inventory listing the vested interest of the estate in the right to receive remaining distribution from the trust. It must be inventoried as a single item and appraised as such by the probate referee. See the Probate Referee Guide at p. 24 (Other Interests). We will accept the referee's appraisal of the preliminary cash distribution, even though the inventory did not characterize it as the decedent's vested right to receive as of the date of death. The inventory and appraisal must be filed conventionally and cannot be e-filed. 1) Leland Valentine was appointed Administrator after a hearing with $300,000.00 bond. The I&A's allege assets of $1,133,209.92. See LRSF 14.16 A. Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond and LRSF 14.11 B. An ex parte petition to waive an additional bond with copies of waivers from all beneficiaries including the guardian ad litem must be filed. A courtesy copy of the petition and proposed order must be provided. An ex parte petition is currently in the filing queue but hasn't been processed. 2) Court to determine whether the sub-trust must satisfy the requirements of Probate Code section 3611(g) and must be identified as such in the order. 3) Petitioner requests a $10,000.04 reserve in a preliminary distribution. Court to determine how to proceed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN JOSEPH GRIFFIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/2021. 1) Please provide a legal basis for bond of $72,000.00 being sufficient when the petition alleges the assets total $2,339,441.63 and the real property has been sold. (See LRSF 14.16 A). 2) Petitioner alleges payments to attorney were made without a court order for trust administration services and $200 for consultation services to Gloria Park. Please provide a legal basis for the executor paying for trust administration services from the estate. Please explain what the $200.00 consultation is for and file a receipt. Please provide a legal basis paying $200.00 from the estate for the consultation. Please provide a legal basis for "the executor will reimburse himself in the sum of $7,700 once the estate assets have been transferred into the trust." 3) Please file receipts for all requests for reimbursement from Michael Kahn on Schedule C: Disbursements. 4) Please explain why all cash has been held in a single account when the FDIC limit is $250,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CARDENAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/11/2021. Filings were not responsive to these issues. Matter continued again to file a proper response. 1) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 2) Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705 (b)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/26/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD L. YOST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That Certain Assets Are Included In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/25/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Neither successor trustee is in San Francisco. How is this the proper venue? Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor's life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Notice with a copy of the petition and supplement (and any other later filed paper) must be personally given to the wife unless she waives such notice. The trust also refers to a Letter of Instructions directing the distribution of personal effects. Was there such a letter? There was also no copy of the death certificate attached to the petition or subsequently filed. This Court requires the bank statements immediately preceding the date of death to be filed to show how title to such accounts was held. (If the copies of the petition that were mailed had copies of the trust attached, then they were not true and correct copies of the petition that was filed. Furthermore, any supplement or amendment to the petition would likewise have to be served. [CRC 7.53.]) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/16/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRREVOCABLE TRUST AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 26, 1981 TRUST

Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Whether Judith Ann Cohen and Andrew L. Cohen should be appointed as co-trustees to serve along Yvonne Anixter Goddard and Marilyn Anixter Allen. 2) Whether to approve the trustee succession plan. 3) Whether the agreement requires the Polk Street Trust to be administer (a) as a single trust in which all the Polk Street Trust beneficiaries have an interest in proportion to their respective shares or (b) as individual separate share trusts in which each Trust beneficiary is the sole beneficiary of a specific separate share trust created for their benefit. 4) Whether 1618-1620-1622 Polk Street should be confirmed as trust property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in Jacqueline Ahwal Petition for Letters of Admin: a. On 06/15/2021 Attorney for Petitioner filed a Notice of Erratum alleging on page 1 2.b. that the inclusion of Joanne Rajae-Tehrani was in error. It appears the petition must be amended. b. Petition does not address whether Jacqueline Ahwal has priority for Letters of Administration.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUZ BERNARDA MANAOIS CERALDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/25/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proof of publication has been filed yet. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is missing the telephone number at item 2. The petition caption is incomplete. Item 3a of the petition is incomplete (place of death). The will was not self-proving. The jurat was for the testator only, not also the witnesses. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. Items 5a(7) or (8) and 5b of the petition were not answered. If there were any such issue or stepchild or foster child, they must be listed in item 8 and must receive notice. Was G.R.C. Gason (Gascon?) the only child of Rhodora Ceralde? The will lists G.R. Ceralde Gascon as one of the testator's children. Did she adopt her grandson? Was Czarina C. Fernandez not survived by issue? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/16/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
